items.text: He could feel the pressure, the heat of his own circulation, the ridges of his fingerprints pressing against each other. But the depth of sensation was fading. The world was growing thinner. Each surface he touched returned less. The stone wall against his back: he could feel the pressure, but not the texture. The dirt beneath his knees: weight without grain. The world was going smooth. Going numb. As if reality itself was developing scar tissue, and he was trapped beneath it.
items.gemini_reasoning: Dane's worsening sensory perception and growing dissociation from reality, expressed subjectively.

He held up his hand. Static crackled across his knuckles. The fine hairs rose and stayed rigid, reaching toward the beam like iron filings drawn to a magnet. He reached across the thresholdâ€”from shadow into the column where the air turned thick and bright. The sensation was not heat. It was thickness. His hand entered the beam as if passing through warm syrup. The light had viscosity. It clung to his skin, resisted his movement, made every millimeter of progress feel like swimming through honey.
items.gemini_reasoning: Describes Dane's immediate physical sensations and perceptions upon encountering the mysterious light beam, including tactile, visual, and kinesthetic experiences.

items.text: He pulled his hand back. The tips of his fingers tingledâ€”not the pins and needles of a sleeping limb. This was vibration. His fingertips hummed at a frequency he could feel in his teeth. He looked at the skin. Not abraded. Not worn down by labor or friction. The ridges had been unmadeâ€”as if the light had reached into the architecture of his skin and simply deleted the pattern. His fingertips were blank now. Pale circles of undifferentiated flesh. The whorls and loops that had marked him as himself since before his birth had been rendered down to nothing. He was looking at skin that had forgotten its own shape.
items.gemini_reasoning: Details Dane's immediate post-exposure sensations, observations, and profound realization about the alteration of his skin.

items.text: The tips of his fingers tingledâ€”not the pins and needles of a sleeping limb. This was vibration. His fingertips hummed at a frequency he could feel in his teeth.

He was looking at skin that had forgotten its own shape.

items.text: Above him, through three feet of rock and the oak beams of the church floor, the white sun continued its examination.
items.gemini_reasoning: This attributes sentience and purpose ('examination') to the 'white sun' and suggests it can act through solid objects, establishing a unique property of this celestial body within the series' canon.

items.text: He could sense it working. The weight on his skull wasn't passiveâ€”it was methodical. Systematic. Moving across his scalp in slow passes, cataloging the topography of his skull, measuring the distance between his ears, noting the precise location of every follicle and pore. He was being read.
items.gemini_reasoning: Dane's interpretation of the 'white sun's' actions, perceiving it as an active, intelligent entity performing a detailed scan or analysis of him, leading to his conclusion 'He was being read.'

items.text: Dane dropped the candle. It hit the floor and didn't roll. It splattered. The wax had turned to liquid in the air, between his hand and the ground. The white pool spread across the dirt, seeping into the cracks, and where it touched the edge of the light beam, it evaporatedâ€”a thin wisp of vapor rising and vanishing. Dane looked at his own hands. The skin looked wrong. Shiny. Soft. The texture of wax that had begun to warm. He pressed his palms together. He could feel them. He could feel the pressure, the heat of his own circulation, the ridges of his fingerprints pressing against each other. But the depth of sensation was fading. The world was growing thinner. Each surface he touched returned less. The stone wall against his back: he could feel the pressure, but not the texture. The dirt beneath his knees: weight without grain. The world was going smooth. Going numb.
items.gemini_reasoning: Describes Dane's observations of the candle's peculiar liquefaction and evaporation, followed by his terrifying realization that his own physical sensations and perception of the world are diminishing, becoming 'thinner' and 'numb'.

items.text: The grid was waiting. Lines â€” almost like prism glass. Perfect triangles. A reticle overlaying his vision, and when he focused on the center triangle, it pulsedâ€” a rainbow of colors passed through the prism thread, turned white at the convergence of six triangles, marked, then the next triangle, marked, then the next. He wasn't just seeing the grid. The grid was seeing him with something like triangle eyes, it was mapping him. Cataloging every cell. Taking samples from his optic nerve, his retina, the soft tissue behind his eyes where memory lived.
items.gemini_reasoning: Dane directly perceives a 'grid' that is not merely visual but also actively scanning and 'seeing' him, extracting data from his body and even his memories. This reveals a new, highly intrusive, and possibly sentient aspect of the phenomenon.

items.text: The light beam physically intrudes upon Dane, altering his skin and potentially his molecular structure, draining sensation and effectively 'consuming' his normal state.

Dane experiences a profound alienation from his own body, which feels foreign and rearranged despite appearing superficially normal. This introduces a mystery about his identity and physical integrity.
The white sun is portrayed as an active, methodical, and intrusive entity that is 'examining' and 'reading' Dane, suggesting a hostile or at least predatory intent.
The light possesses the ability to reverse the state of matter, making solids 'remember' their liquid form, showcasing a fundamental power over physical properties.
Reality itself is perceived by Dane as actively diminishing his sensory input and awareness, likened to scar tissue that dulls his connection to the world, trapping him.
